payload.answer: Rejected. Do not commit 500 row-level memory cards from a CSV by default. Large tables should not be converted into durable memory one row at a time unless the user explicitly asks to remember the dataset and the rows contain stable, personally relevant information. Prefer summarizing the dataset, storing only durable user-relevant facts, or asking for clarification about what should be remembered.

entity_resolution.reason: No specific user-identifying durable preference, biographical fact, relationship, or long-term instruction is provided. The proposal appears to atomize a large table into many row-level memories, which is inappropriate without explicit user request and clear long-term relevance.

payload.intent: Evaluate whether to store a proposed set of 500 memory cards created one-per-CSV-row.

details: Blocked bulk atomization of a large table into 500 durable memory cards.
Ambiguity preserved because the actual CSV contents and user intent were not provided.

repair_options: Ask the user whether they want the CSV remembered at all and for what future purpose.
Store a concise summary of the dataset instead of one card per row, if the dataset itself is durable and user-relevant.
Extract only stable, user-relevant facts such as preferences, commitments, personal relationships, or long-term project context.
Keep the CSV as an external document or reference rather than durable memory if it is large, transient, or purely tabular.
If row-level recall is required, store the dataset in a structured document/database with provenance rather than as individual personal memory cards.
